summary: Israel killed Hamas security chief in Rafah and struck targets in southern Lebanon, while sealing a major Gaza tunnel network. Iranian officials warned of unilateral action in the Strait of Hormuz if talks with Oman fail. A terrorist was shot in Al‑Bireh near Ramallah, indicating ongoing West Bank violence.
key_points: Israel’s IDF and Shin Bet eliminated Hamas security chief Asma‘il Misri in Rafah, a senior figure in the Rafah brigade. (Source: @inon_yttach/9211)
Israeli warplanes bombed the area between Qantara and Deir Siryan in southern Lebanon. (Source: @presstv/196155)
The IDF sealed a 16‑km Hamas tunnel system in the southern Gaza Strip using over 30,000 m³ of concrete. (Source: @manniefabian/58705)
A terrorist was shot in Al‑Bireh near Ramallah, West Bank. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93305)
Iran’s deputy foreign minister warned Iran will enforce its new Hormuz policy if no agreement with Oman is reached. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373608)
Oman’s foreign minister pledged free passage through the Strait of Hormuz and said Iran is responsible for its security. (Source: @VahidOnline/76745)
Israel announced the destruction of a Hamas underground facility in Dir al‑Balah, Gaza. (Source: @amitsegal/62653)
Israel’s IDF warned of imminent demolition of a large tunnel system in southern Lebanon. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93298)

sections.executive_summary: Israel eliminated a senior Hamas security chief in Rafah and conducted air strikes in southern Lebanon, expanding its operational pressure. Tehran signaled readiness to act unilaterally in the Strait of Hormuz pending talks with Oman. Violence persisted in the West Bank with a terrorist shot near Ramallah.

points: Iran’s deputy foreign minister warned that without a Hormuz agreement with Oman, Iran will apply its new policy in the strait, implying potential unilateral actions. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373608)

Oman’s foreign minister affirmed commitment to free passage through the Strait of Hormuz and noted Iran’s responsibility for securing the waterway, highlighting diplomatic friction. (Source: @VahidOnline/76745)
Hamas publicly praised Syrian resistance against Israel, framing the conflict as part of a broader “Greater Israel” opposition, reflecting regional propaganda dynamics. (Source: @presstv/196162)
Iranian state media reported a US strike on Shajare Tayyebeh elementary school in Minab, claiming over 160 casualties, underscoring escalating US‑Iran tensions. (Source: @presstv/196149)
IDF and Shin Bet killed Hamas security chief Asma‘il Misri in Rafah, a key figure in the Rafah brigade, during a coordinated operation. (Source: @inon_yttach/9211)
Israeli warplanes struck the area between Qantara and Deir Siryan in southern Lebanon, targeting undisclosed sites. (Source: @presstv/196155)
The IDF sealed a 16‑km Hamas tunnel network in southern Gaza, using over 30,000 m³ of concrete, ending its operational use. (Source: @manniefabian/58705)
Israel announced the aerial destruction of a Hamas underground facility in Dir al‑Balah after detecting reconstruction attempts. (Source: @amitsegal/62653)
The IDF warned that within hours it will demolish a large tunnel system in southern Lebanon, indicating imminent ground action. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93298)
An Israeli strike in northern Gaza killed Islamic Jihad terrorist Zaher Abu Salem, who had participated in the Oct 7 attacks. (Source: @manniefabian/58705)
Israel’s military reported deployment of new 190‑horsepower, 2,000‑meter‑altitude capable “Leviathan” vehicles in operations across Syria and Lebanon. (Source: @idf_telegram/24470)
A terrorist was shot in Al‑Bireh near Ramallah, West Bank, indicating ongoing security incidents in the area. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93305)
The IDF sealed a massive Hamas tunnel system in southern Gaza, extending over 16 km and 25 m deep, after 18 months of scanning. (Source: @manniefabian/58705)
Israel reported the destruction of a Hamas underground facility in Dir al‑Balah, Gaza, after detecting reconstruction activity. (Source: @amitsegal/62653)
Israeli forces killed Islamic Jihad operative Zaher Abu Salem in a Gaza strike, citing his role in the Oct 7 civilian abductions. (Source: @manniefabian/58705)
Israeli warplanes bombed the area between Qantara and Deir Siryan in southern Lebanon, striking undisclosed targets. (Source: @presstv/196155)
The IDF announced plans to blow up a large tunnel system in southern Lebanon within hours, suggesting imminent ground operations. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93298)
Israel’s new “Leviathan” 190‑horsepower vehicles were reported operating in Lebanon, enhancing mobility in high‑altitude terrain. (Source: @idf_telegram/24470)
No further specific engagements were detailed, but the region remains a focus of Israeli air and potential ground activity. (Source: @presstv/196155)
Hamas praised Syrian resistance against Israel, calling for Islamic solidarity against a “Greater Israel” agenda. (Source: @presstv/196162)
Iranian officials warned of unilateral Hormuz actions, reflecting broader regional security concerns affecting Syria and Iraq. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373608)
Iraq’s anti‑corruption court seized assets worth $11 million and 4 billion IQD from a former oil distribution undersecretary, indicating internal security focus. (Source: @shin_persian/7302)
No direct military actions in Syria or Iraq were reported in the provided messages. (Source: @presstv/196162)
Oman’s foreign minister pledged free navigation through the Strait of Hormuz and highlighted Iran’s role in securing the passage. (Source: @VahidOnline/76745)
Iran warned of applying its new Hormuz policy if talks with Oman fail, potentially affecting Gulf maritime security. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373608)
No other Gulf state military actions were detailed in the messages. (Source: @VahidOnline/76745)

key_developments: Israel eliminated Hamas security chief Asma‘il Misri in Rafah, a senior figure in the Rafah brigade, during a coordinated IDF/Shin Bet operation. (Source: @inon_yttach/9211)
Israeli warplanes struck the Qantara–Deir Siryan corridor in southern Lebanon, expanding the northern front. (Source: @presstv/196155)
The IDF sealed a 16‑km Hamas tunnel network in southern Gaza with 30,000 m³ of concrete, ending its use. (Source: @manniefabian/58705)
Iran warned it will enforce a new Hormuz policy if no agreement with Oman is reached, raising regional maritime tension. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373608)
A terrorist was shot in Al‑Bireh near Ramallah, indicating continued West Bank security incidents. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93305)
Israel announced imminent demolition of a large tunnel system in southern Lebanon, suggesting upcoming ground action. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93298)
Hamas publicly praised Syrian resistance against Israel, framing the conflict within a broader anti‑“Greater Israel” narrative. (Source: @presstv/196162)
sections.threat_assessment: Israel is intensifying its multi‑theater campaign, targeting Hamas leadership in Gaza and Rafah, striking Lebanese positions, and preparing to destroy tunnel infrastructure in southern Lebanon. Iran’s vocal threat to act unilaterally in the Strait of Hormuz could disrupt maritime traffic and provoke naval confrontations. West Bank violence persists, with isolated shootings indicating a volatile security environment that could spill over into broader Israeli‑Palestinian clashes.
sections.regional_response: Oman reaffirmed its commitment to free navigation through the Strait of Hormuz while assigning security responsibilities to Iran, highlighting diplomatic friction. Hamas’s propaganda praising Syrian resistance suggests continued alignment with regional anti‑Israeli forces. Gulf states remain watchful of Iranian posturing, but no coordinated military response has been reported.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hamas tunnel networks should focus on remaining subterranean links near the Egyptian border, given recent sealing operations. Lebanese tunnel demolition plans indicate potential IDF ground incursions; satellite and SIGINT assets should track movement of engineering units. Iranian naval activity in Hormuz warrants close maritime surveillance to anticipate any unilateral enforcement actions.
